visible_observations: A mature blonde woman stands squarely in fitted dark leather trousers, boots, a high-collared long coat or bodice, and an open neckline, holding a large inscribed book against one hip.
Her extended arm is covered in small script, the raised hand is blackened to the wrist, and viscous black drops hang from the fingers; matching script continues across the neck and other arm.
Direct eye contact, an upright spine, planted legs, the forward hand, and the heavy book establish controlled spellcaster authority rather than passive display.
strengths: Adult femininity, a fit body, controlled posture, tome, runic skin, and ink-black hand combine into a readable combat-caster identity.
Scuffed brown-black leather, restrained bronze marks, and dark liquid keep the materials tactile and weathered rather than bright or crystalline.
defects: The blonde updo, corseted leather tailoring, cleavage, long coat, and tall boots form a familiar premium-fantasy witch outfit before they establish an individual culture or survival history.
The runes can read as decorative tattoos and the black hand as an active spell effect, so the image does not clearly distinguish supernatural cost from chosen styling or weaponry.
The polished standing presentation carries less immediate combat danger than the forward hand suggests.
uncertainties: Unaided inspection cannot determine whether the script is imposed punishment, self-authored magic, ownership marks, or ordinary ornament.
The intended target and exact function of the book and black liquid are not visually certain.

visible_observations: Three alternatives show a seated writer behind an oversized open tome, a tall standing woman carrying a large closed book while raising a dripping hand, and a wide standing figure spreading a multi-panel manuscript between both hands.
The alternatives change pose, width, prop configuration, negative space, and the relationship between the figure and the writing surface.
strengths: The seated, vertical, and wide-spread options are materially different compositions rather than minor hand or clothing changes.
Candidate B preserves a clear body silhouette while making the book and lifted hand readable as separate tools.
defects: Candidate A is dominated by chair, cloth, and book mass, while candidate C reads first as a map or banner display; both suppress bodily combat intent.
Across all three options the same slim tailored humanoid body remains conventional, so most distinction comes from book staging rather than anatomy or locomotion.
uncertainties: The sheet does not establish whether the book changes form in use or whether the dripping hand is independently important at silhouette distance.

visible_observations: A conventionally attractive adult face looks directly at the viewer with slightly narrowed eyes, clean arched brows, a closed relaxed mouth, a raised chin, and near-symmetrical features.
Loose hair crosses one temple, runic marks climb the neck, and the rigid high collar frames the face without obscuring it.
strengths: Maturity, direct eye contact, steady head angle, and controlled eyelids communicate composure and appraisal rather than innocence or vulnerability.
The neck markings connect the face to the visible magic system while preserving clear facial anatomy.
defects: The smooth symmetry, softly closed lips, and fashion-portrait lighting produce a controlled beauty-model seriousness more strongly than a specific villainous want, fear, compulsion, or contradiction.
The eyes do not visibly lock onto the extended hand, book, or another target, leaving the pose and face only loosely connected.
uncertainties: The unaided read could be confidence, guarded appraisal, seduction, impatience, or neutrality; no single active survival strategy is visually decisive.

visible_observations: At native size, a tall feminine figure, one extended arm, the rectangular book at the opposite hip, separated legs, blonde hair, and the long dark coat remain visible.
The blackened hand, hanging ink, runic skin, facial expression, tailoring, and most leather wear collapse into the dark figure and background.
strengths: The opposing hand-and-book arrangement preserves a simple caster gesture and prop read at the frozen scale.
Blonde hair and exposed skin provide small warm anchors against the dark costume.
defects: Recognition depends on a conventional dark-coated woman-with-book silhouette because the script and ink mechanism do not survive.
The coat, trousers, and background merge through the lower body, weakening leg separation and material hierarchy.
uncertainties: The native image alone does not establish whether players would identify the black-hand mechanic or distinguish this figure from another slim dark-clad caster.
